concerns.text: Task T013 implements oracle generation on N=200 examples, and T014 enforces variance checks on this subset. This silently weakens FR-002 which mandates 'at least 500 examples' and requires the pipeline to 'fail with an explicit error' if fewer than 500 are found. The task list implements a successful run on a reduced set, violating the spec's hard constraint on sample size and failure logic.

Task T026 implements Spearman correlation against a 'random baseline' but omits the 'uniform-weight baseline' explicitly required by FR-005 and SC-001. The task description does not mention generating or comparing against a uniform-weight baseline, resulting in a dropped acceptance criterion for the evaluation phase.

Task T007 configures hyperparameters for 'N=200 examples' and T008 handles edge cases for 'failed examples' without enforcing the FR-002 requirement to fail if N < 500. This creates a semantic drift where the implementation logic (allow N=200) contradicts the spec's failure condition (fail if N < 500).

Task T013 specifies using 'Phi-3-mini' as the Oracle model. FR-002 and User Story 1 explicitly require 'Llama-3-8B' (or similar) to ensure consistency with the 'known reasoning patterns' defined in FR-003. Using a different model (Phi-3-mini) without a spec amendment violates the semantic requirement for the oracle ground-truth generation.
